summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that objects returned by name handlers can have dangerous methods that completely bypass DISALLOW_FUNCTIONS security checks.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that a name handler returning an object with dangerous methods (run, read_file) can execute arbitrary code and read sensitive files like /etc/passwd, completely bypassing the sandbox.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully demonstrated that name handler returned object methods executed arbitrary code (creating /tmp/pwned_vuln3) and read /etc/passwd, confirming complete sandbox escape.
verification_method: The run script executes the exploit and verifies both arbitrary code execution (via /tmp/pwned_vuln3 file) and information disclosure (reading /etc/passwd), demonstrating complete security bypass.
evidence_produced_by_run: Container logs show name handler returning CodeExecutor object
Container logs show system.run() method executing arbitrary code via eval
Container logs show /tmp/pwned_vuln3 created with content 'hacked_by_vuln3'
Container logs show system.read_file() method reading /etc/passwd
Container logs show /etc/passwd content successfully retrieved
No exceptions or security blocks occurred despite DISALLOW_FUNCTIONS containing eval

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host and mounts it into the container at /target-repo.
